title: Asterisk Queue Log Wait Time Versus SLA Threshold
app: Custom TA, HEC, or Universal Forwarder mapping Asterisk logs to `asterisk:pjsip`, `asterisk:cdr`, `asterisk:queue_log`, `asterisk:full`, `asterisk:messages`, `asterisk:security`, `asterisk:ami`
spl:
index=voip sourcetype="asterisk:queue_log" earliest=-24h
| eval ev=coalesce(event, Event, "")
| eval wait=tonumber(coalesce(wait, data2, Wait, hold_time, ""))
| where match(lower(ev), "(?i)connect|completecaller|completeagent") AND isnotnull(wait)
| eval sla_seconds=120
| eval breach=if(wait>sla_seconds,1,0)
| stats avg(wait) as avg_wait p95=perc95(wait) sum(breach) as breaches count as answered by queue
description: Queue wait drives abandons and poor satisfaction. `queue_log` is authoritative for time-to-answer versus IVR timestamps alone.
value: Aligns staffing to SLAs and highlights dialplan regressions after upgrades.
implementation: (1) Parse queue_log fields for your Asterisk version. (2) Set `sla_seconds`. (3) Alert on p95 or breach rate. (4) Optional join to CDR for abandons.
visualization: Timechart p95; KPI breach %; bar by queue.
grandmaExplanation: We watch this Asterisk system’s calls and health, so trunks, queues, and voicemail stay in shape for everyday use.
knownFalsePositives: Understaffing, sudden spikes, and callback-heavy flows that are operations issues, not PBX health.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
